channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Understanding LEN: HIV Prevention and Community Awareness in South Africa
item.description: The article explores the rollout of lenacapavir (LEN), an HIV prevention injection, and highlights the critical need for public understanding to combat misinformation. The author recounts a personal a...

item.title: Global News Roundup: U.S.-Iran tensions escalate, Bangkok tragedy, and key international developments
item.description: The article compiles a range of global news events from July 13, 2026. Key highlights include the U.S. launching additional strikes on Iran to counter threats to commercial ships in the Strait of Horm...

item.title: Pauline Hanson's One Nation Faces Polling Decline Amid Policy Shifts and AI Government Plans
item.description: The article details the declining support for Pauline Hanson's One Nation party, highlighting her recent UK activities including meetings with far-right figures and vocal supporters. It notes her part...

item.title: Bangkok Pub Fire Claims 27 Lives as Rescue Efforts Continue
item.description: A devastating fire erupted at a pub in Bangkok on July 13, 2026, resulting in the deaths of at least 27 people and injuring several others. Firefighters took approximately half an hour to control the ...

item.title: Phia faces allegations of affiliate fraud over alleged cookie stuffing practices
item.description: Phia, a shopping startup co-founded by Phoebe Gates and Sophia Kianni, is under investigation for alleged 'cookie stuffing' practices that may have allowed the company to claim commissions for sales i...

item.title: Robin Padilla's Role in Impeachment Trial: A Blend of Artistry and Politics
item.description: The article discusses Senator Robin Padilla's approach to the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, highlighting how his persona as an actor influences his political strategies. Despite cr...

item.title: Nigeria banks cut N5.4tn in credit to oil, ICT, and other sectors due to CBN policies
item.description: Deposit Money Banks (DMBs) reduced lending to critical sectors like oil and gas, ICT, construction, education, and manufacturing by N5.45 trillion in 2025, driven by the Central Bank of Nigeria's (CBN...

item.title: Campaign for Access to Enhertu Treatment for Incurable Breast Cancer Patients
item.description: The article highlights the disparity in access to the life-extending drug Enhertu for patients with metastatic breast cancer in the UK compared to Scotland and 26 European countries. Kathryn Hulland, ...

item.title: Cultural safety in healthcare reduces disparities and saves lives
item.description: The article discusses the government's proposed changes to medical regulation in New Zealand, which critics argue undermine cultural safety in healthcare. Cultural safety, a practice rooted in Māori v...

item.title: Lagos State TaskForce Impounds 309 Motorcycles to Enhance Safety and Traffic Flow
item.description: The Lagos State TaskForce conducted a major operation along the Lagos-Badagry Expressway, impounding 309 commercial motorcycles to improve traffic flow, restore order, and strengthen security. This en...

item.title: EU Urges Full Implementation of 2016 South China Sea Arbitral Ruling to Ensure Regional Stability
item.description: The European Union (EU) has reiterated its call for all parties involved in the South China Sea dispute to fully implement the 2016 arbitral ruling, marking the decision's 10th anniversary. The EU emp...

item.title: Morissette Shares Insights on Her Role in 'The Notebook: The Musical'
item.description: Morissette, a renowned singer and actress, has expressed that her role in 'The Notebook: The Musical' holds significant personal meaning for her. The musical adaptation of Nicholas Sparks' beloved nov...

item.title: Jannik Sinner retains Wimbledon title with victory over Zverev
item.description: Jannik Sinner secured his fifth Grand Slam title by defeating Alexander Zverev in a grueling Wimbledon final. The match, which lasted over three hours, saw Sinner overcome a challenging first set to e...

item.title: Balancing Art and Ethics: The Case for Separating Artists' Lives from Their Work
item.description: The article explores the complex relationship between artists and their work, using the example of Rabbi Shlomo Carlebach and Roger Waters. A film about Carlebach's life, titled 'The Darkest Light,' h...

item.title: Saskatoon Resident Takes Initiative to Improve Pedestrian Safety with Crosswalk Paintings
item.description: Derek Cameron, a Saskatoon resident, took matters into his own hands by painting crosswalk lines on Main Street and Lake Crescent after growing concerned about unsafe parking practices near pedestrian...

item.title: Analyst Reflects on Lindsey Graham's Death and His Motivations for Supporting Trump
item.description: The article discusses the death of Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC) and an analysis of his decision to support President Donald Trump. Political analyst Will Saletan, writing for The Bulwark, suggests Gr...

item.title: Democrats Gain Momentum in Maine Senate Race After Candidate Drops Out
item.description: The article discusses how Democrats have gained significant momentum in the Maine Senate race following the withdrawal of candidate Graham Platner. Platner suspended his campaign after multiple allega...

item.title: Marc Marquez extends championship challenge with Sachsenring triumph
item.description: Seven-time MotoGP world champion Marc Marquez secured a dominant victory at the Sachsenring circuit, adding to his sprint race win and strengthening his position in the championship battle. The Ducati...

item.title: Tanzania Prisons Struggle to Secure Premier League Spot After Defeat
item.description: Tanzania Prisons football team faces a challenging situation after suffering a 4-0 defeat to Tanzania Police in the first leg of their crucial playoff match. The loss puts their Premier League status ...

item.title: England's World Cup Potential: Overcoming Challenges to Reach New Heights
item.description: England's football team, led by captain Harry Kane, is striving to reach new heights in the World Cup after a hard-fought quarter-final victory over Norway. Manager Thomas Tuchel expressed dissatisfac...
